content: Hey Saturday, I’m building a personal note-taking setup in Obsidian and I want to make a custom plugin in TypeScript. Goal is to auto-link notes based on entity extraction—people, projects, and dates. Can you help me sketch the architecture?

Nice project—this is super doable in Obsidian’s plugin API. I’d split it into three parts: an entity extraction pipeline, a link resolver/index, and a UI layer that writes links back into notes. Keep extraction async and cached so you’re not reprocessing every file on each vault change.

Yeah that makes sense. I already have basic extraction working with compromise + a few regex rules for dates. I’m stuck on linking strategy though—like how to map "Alex" to "People/Alex Chen.md" reliably when names are ambiguous.

Use a scored resolver: exact alias match first, then path-based hints (folder = People/), then fuzzy similarity with a threshold. Add frontmatter IDs (e.g., `entityId: person_alex_chen`) and keep an alias map in memory for deterministic matches. If confidence is low, log unresolved entities to a review note instead of auto-linking.

Great. I also want it to play nice with Dataview because my weekly review template pulls task + meeting notes. Right now I have a template that should auto-populate from Monday–Sunday, but it misses notes created late at night.

That’s usually a timezone boundary issue—Dataview date parsing can treat file ctime differently than your local day cutoff. In your query, normalize with Luxon and explicitly set local zone, then filter between `startOf('week')` and `endOf('week')` based on your preferred week start. Also decide whether to use file creation time vs a frontmatter `date` field for consistency.
Ah got it. Last thing: I have hover-preview working for some linked entities, but not all—especially links inserted by my plugin after file save. Could be metadata cache timing maybe?

Yep, likely cache timing—after programmatic edits, wait for metadata to re-index before expecting hover to resolve. Trigger your link insertion in a debounced `modify` flow, then call a lightweight refresh once `metadataCache` updates for that file. If needed, queue updates and process sequentially to avoid race conditions when multiple notes save quickly.
